commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a `google-gemini-cli` image-capability fallback in Gateway session utilities for Gemini pro/flash models plus regression coverage for stale text-only metadata and embedding rejection.

PR surface: Source +20, Tests +34. Total +54 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main runs the image-capability resolver before attachment parsing, and a false result produces the reported `UnsupportedAttachmentError`; I did not rerun the live Gemini CLI flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated repair is appropriate because the remaining blockers are contributor-supplied real behavior proof and maintainer selection of a canonical sibling PR.

target.title: fix(gateway): allow Gemini CLI image-capable models
target.body:
Fixes #91739

## Summary
- treat `google-gemini-cli` Gemini flash/pro chat models as image-capable when catalog metadata is stale or missing image input
- preserve fail-closed behavior for non-chat Gemini models such as embeddings
- add focused `resolveGatewayModelSupportsImages` regression coverage

## Real behavior proof
- `pnpm format:check src/gateway/session-utils.ts src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts`
  - `All matched files use the correct format.`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts`
  - 4 test files passed
  - 444 tests passed
- `git diff --check`
target.author: yonganzhang
